Fixes accumulating zombie rclone processes by running tini as the container init.

## Fixed

- **BombVault no longer leaves behind zombie (`<defunct>`) `rclone` processes.** (#35, thanks @strese) BombVault runs as PID 1 inside its container and shells out to restic, which forks its own `rclone` helper for off-site/cloud repos. When a backup or check is cancelled or times out (the off-site check timeout, a manual cancel, a WAN drop, a container restart), restic gets killed and its rclone helper is left orphaned onto PID 1. A plain Go binary running as PID 1 does not reap adopted processes, so those orphans piled up as `[rclone] <defunct>` over time. The image now runs `tini` as its init (PID 1), which reaps orphaned subprocesses automatically (this also covers the analogous virsh-over-ssh orphans from VM backups). It is baked into the image, so there is nothing to configure. As a bonus, `docker stop` is now faster: the container shuts down cleanly instead of waiting out the stop grace period.

To pick up the fix, pull the updated image and recreate the container.

---
